_note: SessionStart hook: injects a tiny luamemo digest into session context so the agent knows what persistent memory + codebase map exist without being asked. Sources ~/.luamemorc (KEY=VALUE via set -a) so MEMO_DB_URL/MEMO_SCOPE reach `memo brief`, then wraps its output as hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext (plain stdout is NOT reliably injected). memo brief is hard-capped by timeout and fail-soft (emits nothing if the DB is unreachable), so the hook never blocks or errors the session.

hooks.command: sh -c 'set -a; [ -f "$HOME/.luamemorc" ] && . "$HOME/.luamemorc"; set +a; d=$("$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/cli/memo" brief 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$d" ] && printf "%s" "$d" | jq -Rs "{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:\"SessionStart\",additionalContext:.}}" || true'
